Active Planned Care / GoalsStart DateReason
Heart failure self management plan02-Feb-2025Chronic congestive heart failure (disorder)
  • Maintain daily weight within 2 kg of baseline to detect early fluid retention.
  • Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg and diastolic below 80 mmHg.
  • Limit sodium intake to less than 2 grams per day.
  • Restrict total daily fluid intake to less than 1.5 liters per day.
  • Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Diabetes self management plan14-Apr-1996Prediabetes
  • Patient will demonstrate understanding of prediabetes and implement recommended lifestyle changes to reduce risk of progression to diabetes.
  • Maintain hemoglobin A1c at or below 6.4% to prevent progression to diabetes.
  • Achieve at least 5% reduction in body weight through diet and exercise.
  • Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular risk.
  • Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Lifestyle education regarding hypertension16-Feb-1986Essential hypertension (disorder)
  • Achieve and maintain blood pressure within recommended target range through lifestyle modifications.
  • Reduce daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams per day to support blood pressure control.
  • Achieve and maintain a healthy body mass index (BMI) through diet and physical activity.
  • Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity per week as tolerated.
Musculoskeletal care30-Sep-1983-
Asthma self management08-Jun-1948Childhood asthma
  • Patient will demonstrate correct inhaler technique and adhere to prescribed asthma action plan to minimize risk of asthma exacerbations.
  • Patient will maintain well-controlled asthma symptoms with minimal use of rescue inhaler.
  • Patient will use rescue inhaler no more than 2 times per week.
Immunization RecommendationDue DateReason
Vaccine product containing only Influenza virus antigen (medicinal product)01-Oct-2025Annual seasonal influenza vaccination
